peripheral_notes.summary: Header pins with documented UART0 data functions; check the pin cautions before use.

notes: J1-3 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label SENSOR_VP.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J1-4 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label SENSOR_VN.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J1-5 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO34.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J1-6 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO35.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J1-7 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO32.
J1-8 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO33.
J1-9 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO25.
J1-10 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO26.
J1-11 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO27.
J1-12 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO14.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J1-13 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO12.
Official LCDKit source shows this pin on the DevKitC connection module.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
MTDI/GPIO12 controls VDD_SDIO voltage at reset unless overridden by eFuse settings.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J1-15 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO13.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J1-16 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label SD2.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J1-17 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label SD3.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J1-18 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label CMD.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J2-2 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO23.
J2-3 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO22.
J2-4 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label TXD0.
Connected to the attached ESP32-DevKitC V4 USB-to-UART bridge.
UART0 is commonly used for boot messages, flashing, and serial debugging.
ROM boot messages print to UART0 by default unless configured otherwise.
J2-5 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label RXD0.
Connected to the attached ESP32-DevKitC V4 USB-to-UART bridge.
UART0 is commonly used for boot messages, flashing, and serial debugging.
J2-6 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO21.
J2-7 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO19.
J2-8 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO18.
J2-9 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO5.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
Controls SDIO slave timing at reset.
J2-10 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO16.
J2-11 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO4.
J2-12 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO0.
Connected to the attached ESP32-DevKitC V4 boot button.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
GPIO0=0 and GPIO2=0 selects Joint Download Boot mode.
J2-13 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO2.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
Controls boot mode with GPIO0.
J2-14 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label IO15.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
Controls U0TXD printing and SDIO slave timing at reset.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J2-15 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label SD1.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J2-16 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label SD0.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J2-17 ESP32 DevKitC connection module pin, silkscreen label CLK.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J5-3 display connection module pin, silkscreen label CS_1.
Display chip-select signal.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
Controls SDIO slave timing at reset.
J5-4 display connection module pin, silkscreen label RESET_1.
Display reset signal.
J5-5 display connection module pin, silkscreen label D/C_1.
Display data/command select signal.
J5-6 display connection module pin, silkscreen label MOSI_1.
Display serial data input signal.
J5-7 display connection module pin, silkscreen label SCK_1.
Display serial clock signal.
J5-8 display connection module pin, silkscreen label LED_1.
Display backlight control signal.
J5-9 display connection module pin, silkscreen label MISO_1.
Display serial data output signal.
J5-10 display connection module pin, silkscreen label T_CLK_1.
Touch-controller clock signal.
J5-11 display connection module pin, silkscreen label T_CS_1.
Touch-controller chip-select signal.
J5-12 display connection module pin, silkscreen label T_DIN_1.
Touch-controller data-in signal. Shares the display serial data path with MOSI in the module schematic.
J5-13 display connection module pin, silkscreen label T_DO_1.
Touch-controller data-out signal. Shares the display serial data path with MISO in the module schematic.
J4-1 SD-card module pin, silkscreen label DAT2.
SD card data 2 signal.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
MTDI/GPIO12 controls VDD_SDIO voltage at reset unless overridden by eFuse settings.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J4-2 SD-card module pin, silkscreen label CD/DATA3.
SD card chip-select or data 3 signal.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J4-3 SD-card module pin, silkscreen label CMD.
SD card command signal.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
Controls U0TXD printing and SDIO slave timing at reset.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J4-5 SD-card module pin, silkscreen label CLK.
SD card clock signal.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J4-7 SD-card module pin, silkscreen label DATA0.
SD card data 0 signal.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
Controls boot mode with GPIO0.
J4-8 SD-card module pin, silkscreen label DATA1.
SD card data 1 signal.
J4-9 SD-card module pin, silkscreen label CD.
Card-detect signal.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.

general_warnings: Confirm the exact module variant before using header GPIOs reserved for on-module memory.
Review each listed caution before wiring a board-header GPIO.

power: The LCDKit board uses a USB power-supply management module and the attached ESP32-DevKitC V4 core board.
Follow the official power-path notes before connecting external 3.3 V or 5 V sources.
programming: The attached ESP32-DevKitC V4 board provides flashing and serial debugging through its USB-UART bridge.
onboard_hardware: ESP32-DevKitC connection module, display connection module, SD-card module, DAC-audio module, and USB power-supply management module.
